message:
fix(hook): canonicalize scratch tempdir path in topology_tempdir helper

`test_build_sync_closure_plan_adds_workspace_metadata_for_member_roots`
failed on macos-arm64 after the /data/projects unpin: macOS routes
`/tmp` through a symlink to `/private/tmp`, so the scratch tempdir
path reported by `tempfile::tempdir().path()` is `/tmp/.tmpXYZ/...`
while the canonicalized path used as the topology policy root is
`/private/tmp/.tmpXYZ/...`. Paths joined off the raw path don't pass
`starts_with` against the canonical root, so the workspace member
was filtered out of the plan and the assertion tripped.

Introduces a small `CanonicalTempDir` wrapper that preserves the
`TempDir` lifetime (auto-cleanup on drop) while exposing the
canonicalized path via `.path()`. All existing test call sites using
`let (temp_dir, policy) = topology_tempdir(); temp_dir.path().join(...)`
continue to compile unchanged and now get consistent topology
semantics across Linux (`/tmp/...`) and macOS (`/private/tmp/...`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
